nodes.title: Cross-User Permission Escalation Audit — 2026-05-19
nodes.summary: Deep test #39 (`deep #42`): cross-user permission escalation against atlas_ui on port 62196. Test users: `perm_alpha_test` (owner), `perm_beta_test` (attacker). IP under test: `cmux_perm_alpha` — created and dispatched by alpha.

nodes.title: DB Concurrent Write Race — Investigation & Fix (2026-05-19)
nodes.summary: `AtlasDB._connect()` was opening SQLite connections in the default `delete` (rollback-journal) mode with no `busy_timeout`. Under concurrent writes from 10 threads this poses a risk: a second writer can receive `sqlite3.OperationalError: database is locked` immediately instead of queuing.

nodes.title: Deterministic Emit Stages (fl-model-gen, cl-model-gen)
nodes.summary: Some pipeline stages do **not** call an LLM. They are mechanical translators that read SSOT and write a Python or JSON artifact whose content is fully determined by the SSOT input. This page explains why that design choice exists, which stages it applies to today, and what the failure modes look like.

nodes.title: Orchestrator System Verification Report
nodes.summary: Cross-reference of all 7 orchestrator wiki pages against the source codebase. Date: 2026-05-18.

nodes.title: Orchestrator Worker Handoff
nodes.summary: This page defines the control-plane concept for cross-workflow repair and pipeline execution.

nodes.title: Orchestrator Worker Handoff — Spec vs Shipped Audit (2026-05-16)
nodes.summary: Review notes for [[orchestrator-worker-handoff]]. Captures the gap between what the page declares as current behavior and what the codebase actually implements, so the doc is not misread as "as-built". Each row of the gap table doubles as a TODO for the orchestrator/gateway build-out.

nodes.title: Orchestrator worker live steps in the detail view — findings + TODO
nodes.summary: > **Date:** 2026-05-23 · **Status:** in progress · **Priority: HIGH** > When you click the orchestrator-chat worker strip chip (`▶ ssot-gen running`), > the detail view must show the worker's **live ReAct steps** (what it's > actually doing), not just a "workflow switched" message. This is the gap the > mockup promised. See…

nodes.title: Orchestrator Workflow Bring-Up (2026-05-17)
nodes.summary: The day `workflow/orchestrator/` went from a missing folder to a real workflow with five differently-modelled workers, an LLM-driven dispatch test against a scratch IP, an append-only trace log, a Pipeline UI debug strip, and a clear list of architectural mismatches the bring-up surfaced.

nodes.title: Parallel TODO Sub-Agent Workers
nodes.summary: Background worker fanout that lets the main agent hand a *batch* of TODOs to `parallel_todo_dispatch(...)` and wait for aggregated results, instead of walking the workflow TODO tracker one item at a time.

nodes.title: Perforce integration 2026-05-30 — Helix Core adapter · Sync UI · test-isolation fix
nodes.summary: Wires ATLAS's provider-abstracted SCM layer to a real Perforce (Helix Core) server and adds a two-pane **Perforce Sync** tab. A developer can push IP folders to Perforce (add → submit) and pull them back (force-sync = overwrite). Built on a local dev server; design is site-portable. Related: [[common-ai-agent-map]] · [[atlas-pytest-hygiene]] (sibling…

nodes.title: Pipeline Progress Debugging
nodes.summary: This page defines how ATLAS should debug progress for pipeline and worker runs without guessing from scattered files.

nodes.title: PL330 Real Orchestrator UI Lessons 2026-05-17
nodes.summary: Real-use debugging record for the `pl330realverify` ATLAS UI run. This page captures the mistakes that only became visible when the user watched the UI and insisted on real Orchestrator + worker execution rather than headless shortcuts or mock progress.

nodes.title: project_classify_survivors_clobber
nodes.summary: This page preserves the historical reference from [[verification-contract-model]] to a known verification hazard: generated classification or survivor artifacts can overwrite evidence from a previous stage if output ownership and freshness are not explicit.

nodes.title: Project Merge Broke Prompt Delivery
nodes.summary: Stub node for the regression class where a merge or frontend/backend wiring change breaks prompt delivery while nearby checks stay green. Treat live "hi" response smoke as the minimum proof before trusting prompt-path changes.

nodes.title: Project Silent Pass Exposure
nodes.summary: Stub node for green-while-broken findings. Use [[silent-pass-exposure-tb-stimulus-gap-20260520]] and [[mutation-baseline-2026-05-23]] for the detailed workflow lesson.

nodes.title: Project TS/Vite/Tauri Cutover
nodes.summary: Stub node for existing wiki references. The active overview remains [[frontend-modernization-2026-05-29]]; this page exists so graph validation has a stable target for cutover-specific notes.

nodes.title: Project Vite Env Stale Build
nodes.summary: Stub node for stale Vite/Tauri build symptoms where code changes are correct but the visible desktop/web surface still serves an old bundle. Verify the active bundle and relaunch path before debugging the wrong layer.

nodes.title: Provider And LLM Call Accounting
nodes.summary: GPT, DeepSeek, GLM, Kimi, Cursor CLI, and Claude CLI may all produce different raw outputs. The workflow must normalize them into the same stage artifact contract before writing files or updating todo state.

nodes.title: QUAD SPI Orchestrator + Multi-Worker + Multi-Sub-Agent Run (2026-05-17)
nodes.summary: Status: pipeline reached `sim-debug` with 60/60 scoreboard rows, 29 goals passing and 31 owner-classified mismatches (28 `rtl-gen`, 3 `tb-gen`). RTL compile/lint are clean and `tb-gen` is green; the remaining work is a real FL-vs-RTL behavior closure loop on the SSOT-derived RTL.

nodes.title: React Flow adoption decision (2026-05-29)
nodes.summary: **Verdict: ADOPT `@xyflow/react` v12 — narrowly (SoC Architect first), and in the NEXT cycle (not now). Decision committed; execution scheduled.**

nodes.title: Round 2 — Retry Budget + Cross-Workflow Routing Validation (2026-05-17)
nodes.summary: Status: end-to-end orchestrator routing flow validated on `simple_uart_tx`. RTL bug injected, kimi `sim_debug` worker silent-failed twice, retry budget exhausted, orchestrator escalated to fallback classification, codex `rtl-gen` applied the repair, and the re-run cocotb suite returned **5/5 PASS**.

nodes.title: RTL-Gen SSOT Contract
nodes.summary: RTL-gen is the SSOT-to-RTL translation workflow. It reads the current canonical SSOT as the mandatory reference and implements that contract in RTL.

nodes.title: Artifact Version And Run History
nodes.summary: Today the UI can show workflow, cost, todo, tool, and intervention history, but it must also show which artifact snapshots a downstream run used. A failed sim is not trustworthy unless it states the exact `ssot`, `rtl`, and `tb` versions it consumed. A failed lint/syn/sta/pnr result is stale if a newer RTL version exists after a repair.

nodes.title: Run Mode And SSOT Provenance Policy
nodes.summary: Status: product/design decision, 2026-05-17.

nodes.title: Session Flow Dashboard
nodes.summary: This page documents the **Session Flow** admin tab: what it measures, how to interpret each metric, the confidence model for historical data, and the runtime no-fanout design that keeps it safe at 100+ concurrent users.

nodes.title: Silent Pass Exposure TB Stimulus Gap 2026-05-20
nodes.summary: Historical context page for the testbench and stimulus gaps that could produce silent-green results.

nodes.title: Sim Debug agent tool — `sim_debug` (VCD + pyslang, drives the waveform UI)
nodes.summary: 2026-05-31. The chat agent can now drive AND query the Sim Debug waveform panel via one tool. It parses the VCD (Python) and the RTL (pyslang) server-side, returns the analysis as text to the agent, and pushes a UI intent the open panel applies (navigate / show signals / cursor / trace).

nodes.title: Sim Debug Feature Review
nodes.summary: Consolidated review of the Sim Debug feature as implemented across the Atlas frontend, backend API, pyslang elaboration layer, VCD tooling, agent tool bridge, and tests. This page is the current high-level operating map; the narrower implementation notes remain in [[sim-debug-module-signals-2026-05-30]], [[sim-debug-agent-tool-2026-05-31]], and…

nodes.title: Sim Debug — RTL module signal panel (2026-05-30)
nodes.summary: Adds a per-module signal browser to the Sim Debug left panel: click a module in the RTL hierarchy tree and the bottom panel lists **that module's signals** — ports (with `in` / `out` / `inout` direction) plus internal nets/variables — filterable by direction, searchable by regex, and addable to the waveform.

nodes.title: Sim Debug Requirements Ledger
nodes.summary: This page consolidates the user-raised Sim Debug requirements from the 2026-05-30 to 2026-06-01 iteration. It is the product/UX ledger; implementation details remain in [[sim-debug-feature-review-2026-05-31]], [[sim-debug-module-signals-2026-05-30]], [[sim-debug-agent-tool-2026-05-31]], and [[sim-debug-waveform-renderer-2026-05-31]].

nodes.title: Sim Debug Waveform Renderer
nodes.summary: Decision: keep the current React DOM plus per-row SVG renderer for now. The current perceived speed is acceptable, and the UI still benefits from simple React-controlled rows, context menus, selection, grouping, and source/wave cross-highlighting.

nodes.title: Single main-loop restoration verify (2026-05-19)
nodes.summary: 검증 목표: ATLAS_SINGLE_MAIN_LOOP=1 로 atlas_ui를 띄우면 단일 main-loop worker (port 5601, --all-workflows) 가 자동 spawn되어 모든 워크플로우(SSOT, RTL, …) 를 순차적으로 받아 처리한다는 것을 cmux Chrome 으로 end-to-end 확인.

nodes.title: SSOT Conversion Flow — Upload → import_manifest → grill-me → to-ssot → validator
nodes.summary: How an uploaded specification (pdf/pptx/docx/md/...) becomes a validated `<ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml`. The end-to-end pipeline is **deliberately split into five stages** so a human can audit each intermediate artifact before the next, heavier stage commits to it.

nodes.title: SSOT Datasheet Authoring — reaching demo-grade quality (2026-05-27)
nodes.summary: > **Updated**: 2026-05-27 > **Scope**: How the SSOT → HTML datasheet (`/api/ssot/export?format=html`, shown in > the interactive SSOT Preview) renders, and how to push a real IP's doc toward the > hand-crafted `interactive_ui/ca53-trm.html` gold standard **by editing the SSOT alone**. > **Reference**: `interactive_ui/ca53-trm.html` (CA53 TRM-style demo) ·…

nodes.title: SSOT-Gen Pass Pipeline
nodes.summary: How `ssot-gen` moves from "LLM produced something" to `status=pass`. This page exists because the QUAD SPI run on 2026-05-17 only reached pass after a deterministic→deterministic→LLM-repair→deterministic→validator chain, and the behavior is not obvious from the stage name alone.

nodes.title: SSOT Import — Multi-Format Markdown Converter (2026-05-19)
nodes.summary: `/api/ssot/import/upload` now accepts PowerPoint, Word, PDF, and HTML in addition to the existing plain-text/markdown formats. The handler keeps the original upload byte-for-byte and emits a Markdown rendering, so downstream `/import` always operates on Markdown.

nodes.title: SSOT Q&A Workbench
nodes.summary: `ssot-gen` should start from the Q&A Session tab. The tab is the authoring surface for missing design decisions, not a passive history viewer.

nodes.title: SSOT RTL Flow Verify 2026-05-19
nodes.summary: Historical cross-link target for the May 19 SSOT-to-RTL product-flow verification work. The detailed evidence lives in the three flow-fix verification pages:

nodes.title: Stage Gate Strictness Audit — 2026-05-19
nodes.summary: **IP under test:** `cmux_r2_gamma` **DB baseline:** `ssot-gen=completed`, `rtl-gen=error` (stale running row also present) **Auditor:** d-gate

nodes.title: Systematic Quality Gates — Manifest Hygiene + SSOT Validator
ATLAS desktop shell (Tauri v2) — MVP runbook
nodes.summary: A native desktop window for ATLAS, for distribution + native file access (OS file dialogs, arbitrary local paths) beyond what a sandboxed browser tab allows.

nodes.title: Tech Direction Recommendation — language / runtime / build (2026-05-29)
nodes.summary: > **STATUS: EXECUTED (2026-05-30)** — the highest-leverage moves in this doc have > shipped: the TSX+Vite cutover landed and the legacy `.jsx` frontend was **retired** > (commits `59cdfb11`, `b6afdae0`), and a **Tauri v2 desktop shell exists** at > `src-tauri/` (`tauri.conf.json` present, Option A webview→backend). The "Current > state" table and…

nodes.title: ATLAS 테스트 방법론 — "green-while-broken"을 막는 4층 구조
nodes.summary: > 핵심 교훈(2026-05-30, 비싸게 배움): **빌드/타입/유닛이 전부 green이어도 실제 앱은 흰화면·무응답일 수 있다.** 조각 테스트는 "부품 OK"만 보장하지, "사용자가 실제로 쓸 수 있다"를 보장하지 않는다. 그래서 **최상층(실브라우저 E2E)** 이 반드시 필요하다.

nodes.title: Triple-LLM `rv32i_min` Experiment
nodes.summary: Reference page for the side-by-side test of three LLM providers running the same SSOT input through the entire common_ai_agent pipeline (`ssot-gen → fl-model-gen → cl-model-gen → equiv-goals → rtl-gen → tb-gen → sim → sim-debug → lint → coverage → goal-audit`).

nodes.title: Truth Coverage Gate
nodes.summary: `truth_coverage` closes the workflow gap where signoff could pass even though the full locked intent was not proven by executable evidence.

nodes.title: uart_tx 직접 end-to-end 실행 — flow 검증 + silent-PASS 실증 (2026-05-30)
nodes.summary: > 핵심 교훈: **이 flow의 골격(사람이 lock한 진실 + evidence-gated + 코드에 박힌 치팅 방어)은 실제로 작동한다.** 약점은 골격이 아니라 "무엇을 관측하느냐(observation depth)"에 있고, 그 약점은 **MSB-first mutation 한 방으로 실물 재현**됐다. 가장 레버리지 큰 보강: audit을 goal *개수*가 아니라 **mutation kill-rate**로 채점.

nodes.title: UI Design References
nodes.summary: Read-only external UI checkouts we study when building or extending ATLAS UI surfaces. None of this code ships in `common_ai_agent`; we only borrow patterns conceptually.

nodes.title: UI Resolution + Theme Matrix Verification — 2026-05-19
nodes.summary: Goal: assert that the ATLAS chat input bar at the bottom of the page and the dir-switcher top bar remain reachable / not clipped across the requested 12 combinations of viewport × view × theme. Owned by worker-14 of the `orch-ux-hardcore` team (handoff `team-plan-orch-ux-hardcore.md`).

nodes.title: Verification Contract Model (VCM)
nodes.summary: Agreed design (2026-06-04) for how a `requirement` is proven against locked truth in the ATLAS workflow. Consolidates the contract-reflection + evidence-contract + semantic-freshness layers into one model. See [[contract-reflection-workflow]].

nodes.title: Wiki Curation Policy — what to capture, when to capture it
nodes.summary: Working policy for `common_ai_agent/doc/wiki/` and the per-IP `<ip>/wiki/` directories. Lives here so it can be revised in place as we learn from real use. Until this page is rewritten, the rules below apply.

nodes.title: Worker / Orchestrator LLM model switch (glm → gpt-5.5 via Codex OAuth)
nodes.summary: Runbook for switching ATLAS off the cost-optimized glm/deepseek worker models onto `gpt-5.5` (≈8× faster per call in measured comparison), and verifying it end-to-end without breaking the running pipeline.

nodes.title: Workflow Feedback And Scheduling
nodes.summary: This page defines how common_ai_agent should run multiple IP workflows without turning the system into either a slow serial script or an unsafe free-for-all.

nodes.title: Workflow Improvement Candidates
nodes.summary: Status: **open ideas, not decided design.** Captured 2026-05-17 after the [[atcuart100-pipeline-run]] and the discussion around what to change next. Each candidate below is a parking-lot entry: problem statement, proposed shape, use cases, and decision questions. None has been promoted to code or to a normative wiki page yet.

nodes.title: Workflow Ownership And Boundaries
nodes.summary: Do not directly patch generated IP artifacts during a pipeline test. Use the owning common_ai_agent workflow, collect evidence, and let validators approve or reject the result.

nodes.title: workspace.jsx Decomposition Plan
nodes.summary: > **STATUS: SUPERSEDED** — workspace.jsx was decomposed into `workspace*.tsx` and > the `.jsx` retired (2026-05-30); kept as historical design rationale.
